reason: Missing dependency edge on node 1.1. Node 1.2.1's load-bearing FIRST step is 'by node 1.1, 1 in A=Im P' — it directly consumes node 1.1's validated result ('1 in A'). Yet node 1.2.1 declares NO dependencies (af get 1.2.1 --full shows no Depends-on line; ledger entry 000024 node_created has no dependencies field). Node 1.1 is NOT an ancestor of 1.2.1 — it is a sibling of the parent (1.2), i.e. an uncle — and a parent's dependency (1.2 Depends on 1.1) does NOT transitively place 1.1 in the proof scope of 1.2's children. So 1.2.1 reaches a node outside its declared scope. This is non-cosmetic and load-bearing: (a) the entire downstream chain attributes '1 in A_+' to node 1.2 (node 1.4(ii): '1 in A_+ ... by node 1.2'), which now rests on 1.2.1, whose very first inference relies on an undeclared 1.1; (b) the linker enforces dependency edges, and the granularity bar set by the VALIDATED siblings is explicit — node 1.2 declares 'Depends on: 1.1', node 1.3 'Depends on: 1.1, 1.2', node 1.4 'Depends on: 1.1, 1.2, 1.3' — each declaring the 1.1 edge wherever it uses 1.1's result. Node 1.2.1 must do the same. The mathematics is correct (1 in A by 1.1; 1 in V_+ by GT-orderunit-def's definitional 'e in A^+' + GT-jb-orderunit-3310, both byte-verbatim in refs/hos/joa-m.md; hence 1 in A cap V_+ = A_+ by def-near-fixed-algebra), so the FIX is a one-line addition: declare node 1.1 as a dependency of node 1.2.1, mirroring sibling 1.2.
